column18: This workload is utilizing greater than 80.0% of the available compute or memory performance of this device. To further improve performance, work will likely need to be shifted from the most utilized to another unit. Start by analyzing DRAM in the Memory Workload Analysis section.

The difference between calculated theoretical (100.0%) and measured achieved occupancy (82.3%) can be the result of warp scheduling overheads or workload imbalances during the kernel execution. Load imbalances can occur between warps within a block as well as across blocks of the same kernel. See the CUDA Best Practices Guide (https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-best-practices-guide/index.html#occupancy) for more details on optimizing occupancy.
